enumerations.orientation_convention: Every labeled A,B,C,D tuple with distinct boundary sites and alternating channel endpoints is checked exactly once: A-B-C-D clockwise or A-B-C-D counterclockwise.
Every labeled A,B,C,D tuple with distinct boundary sites and alternating channel endpoints is checked exactly once: A-B-C-D clockwise or A-B-C-D counterclockwise.
Every labeled A,B,C,D tuple with distinct boundary sites and alternating channel endpoints is checked exactly once: A-B-C-D clockwise or A-B-C-D counterclockwise.

exact_method.actual_odometer_proof: u=q L^-1 e_x is nonnegative and algebraically resets all-3. Least action gives the true odometer v<=u. Then w=u-v>=0 and stability gives Lw<=0. The reduced-Laplacian maximum principle forces w=0, hence v=u.
exact_method.adjugate_formula: L^-1 e_x = adj(L)e_x / det(L). If g_x is that integer adjugate column, the least exact-reset pulse at x is q_x = det(L) / gcd(det(L), every entry of g_x).
exact_method.arithmetic: Pure Python exact integers/Fraction; determinant by fraction-free Bareiss elimination and inverse by rational Gauss-Jordan elimination.
exact_method.common_pulse: For inputs A,B the least common exact-reset pulse is q = lcm(q_A,q_B). Every common reset pulse is m q.
exact_method.parity_filter: At q compute R=[[qG(C,A),qG(C,B)],[qG(D,A),qG(D,B)]] mod 2. Odd multiples preserve R; even multiples make it zero. Therefore a tuple works for some common reset pulse iff it works at q.

general_theorem: For any finite dissipative graph sandpile with reduced toppling matrix L and maximal stable background s_v=L_vv-1, an equal-pulse, exactly resetting two-input parity crossover at fixed ports exists iff the integer Green columns at q=lcm(ord_coker(L)(e_A),ord_coker(L)(e_B)) have the desired 2x2 output matrix modulo 2. If it exists, q is the least pulse and precisely its odd multiples work.

lexicographic_optimum.classification_at_optimum: The 2x2 boundary has one four-site set. All eight alternating labelings (four cyclic starts times two orientations) work, and adjugate denominators force every input reset pulse to be a multiple of 24.
lexicographic_optimum.objective: First minimize square side n, then pulse p, among four-distinct-boundary-port equal-pulse exact-reset parity crossovers.
lexicographic_optimum.reason_no_smaller_side: n=1 has only one boundary site and cannot supply four distinct ports.

model.crossing: four distinct boundary ports with paired endpoints A-C and B-D alternating in cyclic boundary order
model.graph: n by n square grid, threshold 4, every off-grid edge connected to a sink
model.input: equal pulse p at A or B, with exact reset required
model.readout: toppling counts at C,D modulo 2; desired response matrix is the 2 by 2 identity

scope_caveat: The perimeter is a sink. This classifies resetting boundary modules, not internally tileable sink-free gadgets.
title: Exact exhaustive classification of small resetting boundary-port parity crossovers
